title: I Know Why The Caged Bird Screams

jokes.text: See, now that's exactly why people be pushing you down the steps.
jokes.setup: Carlton has just finished his enthusiastic peacock school spirit chant
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt assessment that Carlton's earnestness makes him a target for bullying
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment that establishes the Will/Carlton dynamic - Will's street smart perspective on Carlton's naive school spirit

jokes.text: Like you care. / You see, now that's where you're wrong, my little pet peeve.
jokes.setup: Will dismisses Carlton caring about the game
jokes.punchline: Carlton calls Will his 'little pet peeve' - a play on 'pet' names that's also literally accurate
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels and fits Carlton's verbose character

jokes.text: Oh, yes, sir, he came out to bet $500 on the game.
jokes.setup: Carlton talks about Will's 'fighting peacock' school spirit emerging
jokes.punchline: Will reveals his spirit is purely financial - he just wants to win a bet
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's practical motivations vs Carlton's pure school spirit

jokes.text: Who would do such a thing? / Guess we can rule out Notre Dame.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks who would vandalize their peacock mascot
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Notre Dame (Fighting Irish) wouldn't do it because they're Catholic/moral
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical joke playing on Notre Dame's religious reputation

jokes.text: So, what's it gonna be, regular or grape-flavored Gatorade?
jokes.setup: Carlton claims the coach consults him on important game decisions
jokes.punchline: Will deflates Carlton's importance by suggesting he only gets consulted on trivial Gatorade flavor choices
jokes.explanation: Classic deflation joke that punctures Carlton's self-importance with a mundane reality

jokes.text: None of those guys will leave the locker room...before they rub me for luck. / Sure they not just rubbing you for fun, baby?
jokes.setup: Carlton innocently explains players rub him for luck
jokes.punchline: Will's suggestive interpretation implies sexual motivation
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo joke - functional but not particularly clever or original

jokes.text: It pays to have friends at the urology lab.
jokes.setup: Jazz explains how he found out the quarterback has the flu
jokes.punchline: His connection is at a urology lab, which wouldn't diagnose flu but sounds medical enough
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - wrong medical specialty but sounds authoritative to the characters

jokes.text: Now, why would I steal something I can't sell?
jokes.setup: Will asks if Jazz wants to steal the SCU statue
jokes.punchline: Jazz's criminal logic - only steal things with resale value
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz character moment - his criminal mindset is practical and business-oriented

jokes.text: Well, the Jazz I know didn't need a reason to do something stupid.
jokes.setup: Jazz is being rational about not stealing
jokes.punchline: Will appeals to Jazz's history of irrational behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency joke - Will knows exactly how to motivate Jazz

jokes.text: Daddy, there's a man under my bed and I didn't tell him to hide there.
jokes.setup: Normal concern about an intruder under the bed
jokes.punchline: Hilary's phrasing implies she sometimes does tell men to hide under her bed
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary moment - her oblivious phrasing reveals more than intended about her love life

jokes.text: How are they going to fence all that crushed velvet?
jokes.setup: Discussion of robbery at the Spelling mansion
jokes.punchline: Hilary focuses on the impracticality of selling tacky 70s decor
jokes.explanation: Good Hilary logic - she's concerned about interior design even during crime discussion

jokes.text: Won't we be in the way? / Now, if you'll excuse me, I have to go check my head for molt.
jokes.setup: Vivian asks if 50-yard line seats will obstruct the game
jokes.punchline: Carlton exits to check his peacock head for molting feathers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Carlton is so committed to the mascot role he's adopting bird biology

jokes.text: God, where was this? / 'Mortal Kombat' by Sega.
jokes.setup: Security guard describes gruesome violent scenes to justify alarm system
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's describing a video game, not real crime scenes
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - perfectly timed reveal that deflates the dramatic buildup

jokes.text: So I put on this real sincere face...before I knew it I had this cop convinced I was Sidney Poitier's son.
jokes.setup: Will needed to fool a cop during their statue theft
jokes.punchline: He impersonated being the son of a famous dignified Black actor
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's quick thinking and cultural awareness

jokes.text: Who? / Hello? / Percival, our beloved peacock.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions Percival being in danger
jokes.punchline: Will's confused reaction to Carlton having named the mascot costume
jokes.explanation: Nice character beat showing how seriously Carlton takes his mascot role

jokes.text: Hand me my head. / I'd love to.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks for his peacock head costume
jokes.punchline: Will's response works both literally (handing the costume) and figuratively (wanting to hurt Carlton)
jokes.explanation: Clean double meaning joke with perfect economy - works on both levels

jokes.text: Can't he be like other dogs and quietly lick himself?
jokes.setup: Complaint about guard dog barking all night
jokes.punchline: Hilary suggests the dog engage in typical dog self-grooming instead
jokes.explanation: Typical gross-out humor elevated slightly by Hilary's oblivious delivery

jokes.text: Strudel, schnitzel... / Mercedes.
jokes.setup: Philip tries to remember German command words for the dog
jokes.punchline: Vivian suggests 'Mercedes' - a German car brand, not a dog command
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of the German word guessing with luxury car reference

jokes.text: He's a quick little sucker, isn't he?
jokes.setup: Family is being chased by attack dog through the house
jokes.punchline: Will's casual understatement about the dog's speed during their terror
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor in midst of physical chaos

jokes.text: I think my Aunt Helen dropped him on his head when he was a baby.
jokes.setup: Someone asks 'What happened to Carlton?'
jokes.punchline: Will explains Carlton's personality as resulting from infant brain damage
jokes.explanation: Standard insult humor elevated by specific family reference

jokes.text: Carlton wouldn't miss that game for all the cheese in Philly.
jokes.setup: Will insists Carlton will definitely show up
jokes.punchline: Uses 'cheese in Philly' instead of typical 'tea in China' saying, referencing cheesesteaks
jokes.explanation: Nice cultural reference that personalizes a common saying to Philadelphia

jokes.text: It could be worse. I mean, he could have been dipped in 11 herbs and spices.
jokes.setup: Carlton is trapped/threatened as the peacock mascot
jokes.punchline: Will references KFC's chicken recipe, playing on bird/food connection
jokes.explanation: Clever brand reference that connects to the bird mascot situation

jokes.text: I mean, he could have been dipped in 11 herbs and spices.
jokes.setup: Will says Carlton's kidnapping 'could be worse'
jokes.punchline: References KFC's secret recipe, treating Carlton like a chicken (since he's dressed as a bird)
jokes.explanation: Clever connection between Carlton's bird costume and chicken preparation, good brand reference

jokes.text: He's not exactly Vince Lombardi.
jokes.setup: Coach is panicking and showing poor leadership
jokes.punchline: Will notes he's not like the legendary football coach
jokes.explanation: Generic sports reference, functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: What's that lumpy thing on the back of your neck?
jokes.setup: Coach says 'at least I still have my health'
jokes.punchline: Will immediately questions something that suggests he doesn't
jokes.explanation: Good timing on the health joke deflation, classic setup/payoff

jokes.text: I guess it's just like the American flu, just colder.
jokes.setup: Coach asks what the Canadian flu is
jokes.punchline: Will's logical but nonsensical explanation based on Canadian weather stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist logic playing on Canadian stereotypes

jokes.text: Just ask Flat Nose Gary.
jokes.setup: Jazz warns that Tony the Shovel won't like Will owing money
jokes.punchline: References someone who presumably got their nose flattened by the bookie
jokes.explanation: Good character name that implies violence, establishes stakes for Will's gambling debt

jokes.text: And if they don't have it here, you gonna get it there.
jokes.setup: Coach explains team needs confidence 'here' (pointing to head) and 'here' (pointing to heart)
jokes.punchline: Will threatens to hit him 'there' while pointing, continuing the gesture pattern
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback that subverts the inspirational speech pattern

jokes.text: Well, I got me a little rumpus in that room the other night, you know.
jokes.setup: Will identifies the location as the Kappa Omega Psi sorority rumpus room
jokes.punchline: He had a 'rumpus' (sexual encounter) in the rumpus room
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay on 'rumpus' with double meaning, though fairly obvious

jokes.text: Restraining order. It's a long story.
jokes.setup: Jazz can't go to the sorority house to help rescue Carlton
jokes.punchline: He has a restraining order (implying past inappropriate behavior)
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing Jazz's history of poor judgment with women

jokes.text: Only if he hasn't eaten.
jokes.setup: Family asks if the escaped guard dog is dangerous
jokes.punchline: Implies the dog will eat people if hungry
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with great economy - maximum threat in minimal words

jokes.text: My God! Somebody stole my Mercedes.
jokes.setup: Philip has been worried about security and installed elaborate alarm system
jokes.punchline: His car gets stolen while he's focused on home security
jokes.explanation: Good ironic payoff to the security subplot - also callbacks the Mercedes reference

jokes.text: But you're awfully cute when you grasp at false hope.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks if Amanda will let him out of the cage
jokes.punchline: Amanda finds his desperation endearing while refusing to help
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor line that reveals Amanda's cruel streak with eloquent phrasing

jokes.text: Dweeb? / Well, I was going to say loner, but that too.
jokes.setup: Carlton starts to reveal he was a 'loner' in high school
jokes.punchline: Amanda jumps to 'dweeb' and Carlton sadly agrees
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's self-awareness and Amanda's bluntness

jokes.text: Well, I'm late for the game. Bye.
jokes.setup: Carlton's heartfelt speech about being a loner who found belonging
jokes.punchline: Amanda's complete dismissal and casual exit
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Carlton pours his heart out and gets completely shut down. The casual 'Bye' is perfectly dismissive.

jokes.text: I think you're taking this jail thing just a little too far, buddy.
jokes.setup: Carlton desperately asks Will to take off his pants
jokes.punchline: Will misinterprets this as sexual prison behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard prison/sexual humor, functional but predictable

jokes.text: Gets a little funky in there too, huh?
jokes.setup: Will is putting on Carlton's sweaty mascot costume
jokes.punchline: Comments on the unpleasant smell inside
jokes.explanation: Generic gross-out humor about sweaty costume - low-hanging fruit

jokes.text: You big bunch of babies.
jokes.setup: Football team is demoralized without their mascot
jokes.punchline: Will calls grown football players babies
jokes.explanation: Simple insult humor elevated by Will's street-smart perspective

jokes.text: Their nose.
jokes.setup: Will talks about fans having hopes, dreams riding on the game
jokes.punchline: References Jazz's bookie 'Tony the Shovel' who will break his nose
jokes.explanation: Good callback with excellent economy - one word delivers the threat

jokes.text: Let's be honest, we don't even know if half you all are gonna graduate.
jokes.setup: Will gives increasingly depressing motivational speech about players' futures
jokes.punchline: Suggests many players might not even graduate, ultimate academic failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Will's accidentally demoralizing speech - hits their deepest insecurity

jokes.text: That's right, you guys, it doesn't matter what we do, we're losers.
jokes.setup: Will's speech has completely demoralized the team
jokes.punchline: Coach accidentally agrees with the negative assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing coach's incompetence and confusion

jokes.text: I escaped while the housekeeper was changing the newspaper.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains how he escaped captivity
jokes.punchline: Implies he was kept like a pet bird with newspaper lining his cage
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist joke that ties into both the bird theme and Carlton's treatment

jokes.text: and you strut like you've never strat.
jokes.setup: Will tells Carlton to strut like never before
jokes.punchline: Accidentally creates nonsense word 'strat' while trying to say 'strutted'
jokes.explanation: Good Will Smith character moment - his enthusiasm creates verbal fumbles

jokes.text: What, powerful hind legs with a colorful tail?
jokes.setup: Amanda says their children would be strange (Peacock/Mustang mix)
jokes.punchline: Carlton describes their imaginary children with actual animal traits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment where Carlton takes the mascot metaphor completely literally - highly quotable and character-specific

jokes.text: He's a quick little sucker, isn't he?
jokes.setup: Presumably the guard dog has returned
jokes.punchline: Will repeats his exact line from the earlier chase scene
jokes.explanation: Good callback that bookends the episode with the same dog situation

tmdb_overview: Bird is the word when Carlton, ULA's peacock mascot, is kidnapped by a rival school before the big game.
